title: AUCE — American University of Culture and Education
evidence_summary: Homepage/navigation lists Graduate links for MBA Program and Master of Computer Science; homepage faculty cards mark Business and Arts & Sciences as Undergraduate & Graduate. Used as navigation signal only, not standalone program evidence.

title: Academic Programs
evidence_summary: Official academics page lists two graduate programs: Master of Business Administration and Master of Computer Science. Both are listed as 39 credits. The MBA is shown as Full-Time (2 Years); MCS is listed as Full-Time. The page also lists graduate admission requirements: official university transcript(s), completed graduate application, 2 recommendation letters, $50 USD application fee, and English Entrance Exam or TOEFL 97 / IELTS 7.

title: Admissions
evidence_summary: Admissions page states Masters Programs admission requirements for MBA and Master of Computer Science applicants, including official transcripts, online application, 2 recommendation letters, $50 USD application fee, and English Entrance Exam or TOEFL 97+ / IELTS 7.0+. It also lists scholarship/financial aid categories.

title: Contact / FAQ
evidence_summary: Contact FAQ states that graduate programs require TOEFL 97+ or IELTS 7.0+. The contact form includes a Graduate Admissions inquiry type.

title: Faculty of Arts & Sciences
evidence_summary: Faculty page describes undergraduate Arts & Sciences majors and includes navigation text to Explore Master of Computer Science. Used as supporting navigation only.

title: Faculty of Business Administration
evidence_summary: Faculty page describes undergraduate business majors. It is not used as standalone graduate-program evidence.

title: Campuses
evidence_summary: Campus page states Beirut Campus has a full range of undergraduate and graduate programs. This is campus context only, not specific graduate-program evidence.

title: About AUCE
evidence_summary: About page states AUCE is licensed/recognized in Lebanon under government decrees and lists MBA Program and Master of Computer Science in footer navigation. Used for institution context and official-site verification.
